title: Canonical Person
description: A canonical person record. Forward-declared in v0.1.0 (Phase 0); active canonicalization begins in P0.2 with the Science Layer migration (186 scholars) and Bosworth rulers fix-up (rulers inline → person PID linkage). Phase 0 records MAY exist as placeholders referenced by other entities; full validation strictness applies from P0.2.

@id.description: Persistent identifier in the iac:person- namespace.

@type.description: Person subtype(s). Multiple allowed (a person may be both Scholar and Mufti, both Ruler and Patron). Each entry requires the supertype iac:Person to be present.

kunya.description: Filiation form (Abū <Name>). Convention: ALA-LC romanized, e.g., 'Abū Bakr', 'Abū al-'Abbās'.

nasab.description: Genealogy chain (b. <father> b. <grandfather> ...). Free-text in ALA-LC romanization.

nisba.description: Geographical/tribal/professional epithet(s), e.g., ['al-Bukhārī', 'al-Ḥanafī']. Order: most specific first.

birth_temporal.description: Date of birth. Often only year-precise; many pre-modern figures only have an approximate date. Use approximation='circa' or 'before' / 'after' liberally.

death_temporal.description: Date of death — far more often attested than birth in pre-modern Islamic biographical sources.

floruit_temporal.description: Approximate period of activity, used when birth/death are unknown. Set approximation='floruit'.

birth_place.description: PID reference to iac:place- record of birthplace.

death_place.description: PID reference to iac:place- record of place of death.

active_in_places.description: Places where the person was professionally active (taught, ruled, wrote, built). PID references.

affiliated_dynasties.description: Dynasties this person was affiliated with — as ruler, vizier, court scholar, patron client, etc. The nature of affiliation is captured in the inverse pointer on the dynasty record.

authored_works.description: Works authored by this person. Bidirectional with work.authors.

teachers.description: Teachers in the isnād / scholarly chain. Important for hadith narrators and madrasa scholars.

madhab.description: PID reference to iac:concept- record of legal school (madhhab). Forward-declared; concept namespace activates in P1.

tariqa.description: PID reference to iac:concept- record of Sufi order. Forward-declared; concept namespace activates in P1.

profession.description: Professional/social roles. Controlled vocabulary; multi-valued. Closed enum for Phase 0; extensible in P1.

$comment: Phase 0.2 hard rules: (a) at least one of birth_temporal / death_temporal / floruit_temporal MUST be present; (b) bidirectional consistency teachers/students enforced post-canonicalization; (c) authored_works ↔ work.authors enforced.
